blocks.content: Rider of Iron Wall exhaled.

blocks.speaker: Rider of Iron Wall
blocks.content: Phew!
Moments later, his electric barrier shattered like glass.
Blood spurted diagonally from his shoulder to his waist, and his upper body slid to the ground.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Defense magic is done.
Gaold pulled his dislocated shoulder back into place.
For someone with heightened sensitivity to pain, it was excruciating, but his eyes scanned his surroundings.
The remnants of Cage B Team moved like a storm, but it was clearly a coordinated effort.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: What are they aiming for?
The dealers hadn't used their strongest attacks yet.
They knew that ordinary attacks wouldn't work.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Are they betting on the Terminator?
That was likely accurate.
They wouldn't include more than two irregulars in a Cage-level team.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Which means…
This was their last chance.
Cage B Team was aiming for one perfect attack, and if Gaold could block it, the victory would be his.
With that thought, Gaold's lips curled into a smile.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Talking nonsense.
Gaold aimed at the metal mage Roche.
Bang! Bang! Bang!
The air gun fired explosively, and Roche hastily erected a metal barrier.
Clang! Clang! Clang!
The deafening noise created air bullet marks on the steel surface.

blocks.speaker: Roche
blocks.content: Grrr!
He kept thickening the barrier, but the air bullets quickly chewed through the metal.
The metal barrier, crushed like clay, finally shattered, and dozens of holes pierced Roche's body.
It took just one second for the air gun to penetrate the 20-centimeter-thick barrier.
But that one second bought Rose enough time to approach Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: This will do.
Pleasure Magic.
A high-level scent magic that stimulated the brain to evoke the most intense nostalgia.
Gaold's face twisted cruelly.
The already fragile emotional impact grew, tormenting him.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Grrr!
The gates of hell in his heart opened, and his extreme endurance turned the surroundings into hell.
Though it made the already violent beast even wilder, Rose had a different plan.
They couldn't overpower Gaold with force. Instead, they would excite him to create a cognitive blind spot.
That blind spot was the only opening to subdue Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: Roche!
But Rose overlooked one crucial fact.
Though it wasn't intentional, Roche's death was a perfect trigger for Gaold.
Tarvan, who had been friends with Roche since childhood, was consumed with rage, and that emotion clashed with Gaold's fury, creating a new gap.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Guh!
Rose's neck was severed by a blade of wind, and Gaold's upturned white eyes locked onto Tarvan's movements.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It's over.
Horkin moved towards Gaold, separate from the plan.
Tarvan and all the mages present were young and talented.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: For the future of the kingdom, this must end here.
Horkin activated his explosive magic.
Suicide, a spell that caused a temporary magic assimilation effect by self-detonation, unleashed a powerful explosion that swept the area.
Unlike the Battle Arena, the scale wasn't reduced, so the radius was immense.
Cage B Team, having quickly moved away, stared blankly at the smoke-filled area.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: He's alive.
Gaold stood there, blood dripping from his forehead, looking displeased.

blocks.speaker: Saint Morak
blocks.content: He's alive.
The mission had failed.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: There's only one thing left.
Communication mage Weygan spoke.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: Do we go all the way, or do we retreat?
They had no intention of retreating.
But Horkin's wish for some to survive weighed on their minds.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: We go all the way.
At Tarvan's words, insect mage Beam stepped forward.

blocks.speaker: Beam
blocks.content: We need to report to the higher-ups. Not everyone can go.
Pick anyone. I'll stay.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: I'll go. I'm not much help in combat anyway.
No one blamed Weygan. To be part of Cage B Team, one had to have pride.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: Let's go.
As the remaining members turned, Gaold flew towards them.
He realized further combat was pointless.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You were a great team. For what it's worth.
Tarvan's eyes filled with killing intent.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: You don't really think it's over, do you?
If he could withstand Suicide, Kaiser Blast wouldn't work either.
But he had to try. That was the Terminator's way.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Stop.
Gaold relaxed his body and stood straight.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: The game is over. I let you have your fun, didn't I?
Game? Fun?

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Vibrations gathered in Tarvan's hands.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: And… you can't go back anyway.
Cage B Team looked puzzled, and Weygan asked.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: We can't go back? Why?
You planned to return using the Meta Gate provided by the association, right?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No one answered, but there were no secrets from Gaold, the former head of the Magic Association.
Miro's dimension is probably gone. The Meta Gate coordinates would be disrupted.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: What are the chances Miro's dimension is gone?
Saint Morak asked.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Not 100%, but over 80%.
That high?

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: Cage B Team began to murmur.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: According to my information, Miro was captured by Archangel Kariel. The Archangel of Creation would have found a way.
Miro was in Heaven.
The new information complicated everyone's thoughts.
If Miro's dimension was destroyed, Heaven's army would come to Earth.
The association would soon learn this, but by then, it would be too late. Every second counted.

blocks.speaker: Morak
blocks.content: We have to check.
Morak looked at the team, and Tarvan asked.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: How?

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: We have to go in ourselves.
So it's a matter of who goes?

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: I'll go.
There were no objections, and Weygan activated the Meta Gate.
Standing before the black sphere, Weygan looked back at his comrades.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: If the coordinates haven't changed, I'll come right back. Be ready to leave.
With such a plan, even one minute was tight.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Come back quickly. Don't get sidetracked.
Ha ha! Who knows.

blocks.speaker: Weygan
blocks.content: Weygan gave a cute smile and entered the Meta Gate.
ΓÇö A minute passed, and he didn't return.
The Great World War.
A small magic circle appeared on Miro's crown and seemed to penetrate her mind.
Miro's brow furrowed slightly.
The restraint was on a different level from human-made magic control devices.
It was a dangerous magic circle that could explode just by entering the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why don't they kill me? The dimensional wall should be gone.
Kariel sneered.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Don't worry. It'll be over soon.
Even with Miro's dimension destroyed, Heaven's army wouldn't move without Anke Ra's permission.
To annihilate the detestable humans, an independent force was needed.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: So Miro was still useful.
The Archangel Council had already been convened. If he could get the opinions of the eight archangels, he could gather enough force to destroy humanity.
With Gaold's attack on Heaven, even Anke Ra wouldn't be able to remain inactive.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Look forward to it. I'll prepare a grand execution.
Kariel left for the Archangel Council with a cold smile.
Even with her life hanging by a thread, Miro showed no fear.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Not killing me means the final war is postponed. But why?
She still didn't know.
To realize something and start acting, she needed someone's help.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Gaold has come?
Then Sein would be with him.
She had caused Gaold a terrible tragedy with her mistake, but with her dimension destroyed, she could only hope they would complete their mission.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: But still…
Arius whimpered and rubbed his face against his restrained master, feeling sorry for her.
Miro petted his chin like a puppy and raised an eyebrow.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: What have these fools been doing for 20 years?
The Seventh Heaven, Araboth.
Ikael sat on the floor, meditating quietly.
Ashur had gone to gather information on Kariel's actions but hadn't returned yet.
Even he, who could manipulate signals freely, found it difficult to observe the Great World War without Kariel noticing.
It would take much longer than usual, as he had to assemble information using signal interference.
Ikael's anxiety grew.
Something unusual was happening in Purgatory. What was going on?
As she sighed softly and calmed her mind, a voice echoed in her head.

blocks.speaker: Anke Ra
blocks.content: Ikael.
Her eyes snapped open.

blocks.speaker: Anke Ra
blocks.content: Sinner Ikael, you are summoned.

blocks.speaker: Anke Ra
blocks.content: The time has come for an answer.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: An answer?

blocks.speaker: Anke Ra
blocks.content: Come before me. Your sins are forgiven.
Her eyes, wide open, began to shine with a light as brilliant as the Milky Way.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Finally…
The time she had spent in penance, her powers restrained, for her unforgivable sin, had passed quickly.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: She felt neither excitement nor joy.
Her only thought was to reclaim her position as the Archangel and restore order to everything that had been disrupted.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Understood.
Ikael stood up abruptly and left the room.
Her body blurred as she soared to the high spire of Araboth in an instant.
